reviews.review_description:
I love the (usually) chill platforming the game offers, with a small mix of coin collecting in each level to make it a bit better. Though the mechanics work ok, the inability to move the level diaramas with BOTH hands makes the camera something that often becomes a huge hassle when playing seated (because how many play games like this standing).
You can only move and push/ pull the levels with your left hand, which utterly limits control of the camera view that you need more and more as you get further in, so if it's not causing many unnecessary deaths, it's otherwise just infuriating.
Either both hands should be able to control it like every other similar VR game, or at least add thumbstick controls to move the levels around...
I know it's a budget game, but that doesn't excuse very poor control schemes in the slightest.
Outside of poor (if not odd) decisions made for some of the controls, it's a delightful very light platformer that's great for some short relaxing (when the camera isn't making you scream vulgarities) gaming session that's great for all ages (even if more geared towards a young audience). 

I definitely recommend it, in need of some work or not.

Controls r tad difficult to get used to especially the pointing w right and jumping w right also and w grip button. Not bothered enough to refund and cute and definitely cheap enough to keep going. Just maybe work on controls maybe jumping w button and there is a part n chapter 1 I’m stuck at boss not sur what to do can u help out there just can’t get past him caus no where to jump or go just running away n circles not sure how to get past or where. But definitely cute cute cute and hope for possible control updates but great job other then that 😍🥰

I cannot praise this title more. It's an incredibly unique and fantastic puzzle platformer. Although it can be played in VR, it really comes alive in MR. The game teaches you the mechanics slowly, allowing the player to acquire skills to complete the levels with minimal frustration before ramping up the complexity. And it's just stunningly beautiful. The little princess will even look directly at you when you move the maze closer to your face. The only thing I find might need polish is the manner which you rotate the levels in front of you while playing seated, but that is such a minor, subjective issue do not let it dissuade you. I've been in VR since 2016 and have played & tested hundreds (if not thousands) of titles--this is such a fantastic, well-made, addictive game it absolutely deserves wide acclaim. I picked it up on a whim (it was incredibly inexpensive) and I honestly didn't expect much. Little Princess is a top-tier game that far outshines popular titles that routinely get highlighted on the store. Just buy it and give it a try. It's a true 10 out of 10.
At $1 this great app has a lot of bang per buck. This is a gift from the gods. Thank you, Sir.
